sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: Let me check what changed after the UP action - did the green block move?

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed after UP - look at green object position
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ['N', 'W']:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, bbox=({min(rows)},{min(cols)})-({max(rows)},{max(cols)})")

# Also check the blue squares
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ['b', 'c', 'g']:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, bbox=({min(rows)},{min(cols)})-({max(rows)},{max(cols)})")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The green block (N) moved from bbox (49,32)-(51,35) to (45,32)-(47,35) - it moved up by 4 rows. The white strip (W) also moved from (48,32)-(48,35) to (44,32)-(44,35) - it moved up too. So both the green block and white strip move together when pressing UP.
The blue squares and the horizontal bar stayed in the same place. So the green block is the player, and the white strip seems to be attached to it (maybe a trail or indicator).
Let me understand the goal. There are three blue squares with charcoal frames and one horizontal blue bar with gray interior. Maybe the goal is to navigate to these objects or interact with them.
Let me try moving toward one of the blue squares to see what happens. The center blue square is at (36,32)-(39,35). The player is now at (45,32)-(47,35). So I need to go up to reach it.
Let me try moving up more to reach the center blue square.
